metadata.title:
VICTIM Of Up.
DEATH TRAP


CROSSING

pages.raw_text:
u. hammers, laborers. waws ter
ribly mangled and killed by a
Northern Pacific passenger train
about O'Clock yesterday after
noon. The tragedy occurred at the
crossing of Fifth av. and Cannon st,
A freight train on the sidetrack had
been cut in two SO pedestrians could
cross the street. AS the passenger
was heard approaching, the head
end of the freight train was backed
down to couple up, preparatory TO
pulling out.


hammers stepped between the
cars Just as they began to close up.
He hurried through the gap and
walked squarely in front of the ap-
preaching passenger. Neither he
nor the engineer had time to make
a. move. The victim was Knocked
more than 100 feet and his body
horribly mangled.


Coroner Witter, who is also com
pany physician for the Northern Pa
ciic, was called and ordered the re-
mains taken to the morgue OF
Buchanan & Co.


hammers relatives are unknown.
His name was written on note
book. His pockets contained
pouch in which was so cents, and
an unopened snuttDoX.


The fact that the crossing is UNI
protected draws attention to the
Northern Pacific's criminal careless
ness in this respect. This railroad
has crossings through the city and
none of them are protected by prop
er gates or watchmen. Some months
ago Commissioner ONO notified the
company that crossings must be
fixed up so as to protect the public
from such accidents. The com
pany promised to look after this.
and the matter was dropped with
the promise Nothing in the way
of recommendation from Coroner
Witter may be expected. During the
last few months there have been
several deaths at the crossings.
